name: Jira Defect Flow
description: End-to-end orchestration for a Jira defect: pull the ticket, triage, hand off to the `bug-fix` skill for reproduction-first / root-cause / minimum-diff discipline, open a PR with the Jira key in the Closes footer, comment + transition the ticket via the `jira` skill, and optionally run the consumer repo's dev-deploy hook. Pure choreography — composes existing skills by name, never by path.

deps._runtime_contract: Sibling skills are addressed BY NAME at runtime. The agent's harness resolves the name to whatever install location the user picked. `source` below is install guidance only — never a runtime path.

skills.name: jira
skills.purpose: All Jira reads, comments, attachments, and transitions. The defect-flow skill never makes a raw Jira REST call.

skills.name: bug-fix
skills.purpose: Reproduction-first, failing test, root vs symptom, minimum diff, regression test, commit-body explains why, tracker loopback (step 8). Defect-flow stages 6–7 are the Jira-specific implementation of step 8.

agents.name: adversarial-reviewer
agents.purpose: Default review pass after gates. Required.

agents.name: security-reviewer
agents.purpose: Run when the diff crosses a security boundary (auth, secrets, user input, deserialization, file/network I/O, dependencies, LLM/agent code).

agents.name: quality-engineer
agents.purpose: Run when the diff introduces meaningful new test surface or new logic.

input.arguments: a Jira issue key (e.g., PROJ-123), optionally with a target environment or deploy hook override

env.DEPLOY_DEV_CMD: Optional. Shell command this skill runs to deploy the merged fix to the dev environment. If unset, the skill stops at PR-merged and asks the user how to deploy. Consumer repos may instead provide an executable `.context/deploy_dev.sh`.
env.JIRA_DEFECT_FIX_PREFIX: Optional. Branch prefix override (default: `fix`). Use e.g. `bugfix` or `hotfix` if your repo convention differs.

artifacts: git branch fix/<key>-<slug> with one or more commits
Jira comments and a transition on <KEY>
